title: Knowledge Event
description: Event stream schema for the knowledge graph. Events flow from pipeline orchestrators through the event-publisher into the knowledge graph, where the knowledge-manager routes them to subscribers and entity records.

eventId.description: Unique event identifier. Format: EVT-{YYYY}-{MM}-{DD}-{HASH} where HASH is a 6+ character alphanumeric string

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the event occurred

eventType.description: Classification of the event. Determines routing to subscribers and broadcast channels.

severity.description: Event severity level. Events at ALERT or CRITICAL are written to broadcast channels in addition to the event stream.

agent.description: Specific agent within the pipeline that generated the event

entityId.description: Entity ID associated with the source context (property, fund, etc.)

phase.description: Pipeline phase that generated the event

orchestrator.description: Orchestrator that routed the event to the event-publisher

title.description: Short, descriptive event title. Must be specific enough to be actionable without reading the summary.

summary.description: Detailed event summary. Should contain: what happened, why it matters, what changed, and any immediate implications.

entityId.description: Entity ID affected by this event

impact.description: How the entity is affected: DIRECT (entity data changes), INDIRECT (related entity impacted), INFORMATIONAL (awareness only)

fieldsAffected.description: Dot-notation paths to entity record fields that should be updated (e.g., 'currentPerformance.occupancy.physical')

affectedEntities.description: Entities affected by this event. The knowledge-manager uses this to update entity records.

data.description: Event-specific structured data. Schema varies by eventType. The knowledge-manager extracts field updates from this object.

tags.description: Freeform tags for subscription matching and rollup classification. Tags should include: market name, property type, risk category, theme.

expiresAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this event expires. Null = never expires. Default TTL is 1 year for most events. DECISION events never expire.

retentionPolicy.description: STANDARD = 1 year, EXTENDED = 3 years, PERMANENT = never expires. DECISION and POLICY_CHANGE events default to PERMANENT.

parentEventId.description: If this event is a follow-up or correction to a prior event, reference the parent event ID

correlationId.description: Shared identifier for related events across pipelines (e.g., all events from a single quarterly review cycle)

broadcastChannels.description: Broadcast channels this event was published to. Populated by event-publisher for events with severity >= ALERT.

action.description: Action taken in response to the event

acknowledgements.description: Subscribers that have acknowledged this event. Populated by knowledge-manager.
